reviews.review_msg: Very intelligent. His h-indexes and the sheer volume of his publications speak for themselves. He does not provide blunt feedbacks but his grading can be really tough. What might be considered as "good work" in other courses would only constitutes as "satisfactory" in his. One simply cannot bullsh*t him.

Professor Howlett is nice and is clearly knowledgable on public policy design. However, taking 854 was one the biggest regrets of my university experience. The professor never gave out any kind of rubrics for assignments, and you had to beg through emails to get any kind of information + Information was vague and changed randomly during the term.

This class gave me mental health problems.....

Howlett is clearly a brilliant writer but should never be allowed to teach. He doesn't believe in rubrics, or really any clear written criteria. You have to find out vital information for the course through either begging him on Gmail or through a classmate. AVOID if you care about your mental health
The best public policy professor in BC
Good professor but seriously, 80% for a paper is a bit too much if you ask me.
Extremely well read and smart guy. Don't try to BS him. Fair marking system. You get the mark that's warranted by your effort and quality.
Professor Howlett is a leader in his field, Public Policy. The material is as dry as it gets but he is an extremely helpful, fair, and straightforward prof. Highly recommended.
